description_long: This object is a disassembled double-barrel shotgun, featuring two long, parallel barrels and a wooden stock with a classic, slightly curved design. The barrels are dark, likely blued steel, and are separated from the main body, lying crossed on the ground. The wooden stock and fore-end display a rich brown color with visible grain, and the metal receiver and trigger assembly are also detached and visible. The trigger guard is rounded and metallic, and the action mechanism is clearly break-action, as evidenced by the separated components. The overall appearance suggests a traditional hunting or sporting shotgun, with robust construction and a utilitarian finish.
description: A disassembled double-barrel shotgun with a wooden stock, dark metal barrels, and a break-action mechanism, showing all major parts separated and laid out.
description_view_0: The shotgun is seen from above, highlighting the crossed barrels and separated wooden stock, with the trigger guard and action parts clearly visible.
description_view_1: A low, side view emphasizes the length of the barrels and the alignment of the disassembled components, with the stock and fore-end in the foreground.
description_view_2: This angle shows the shotgun from the opposite side, revealing the curvature of the stock and the arrangement of the action and trigger guard parts.
description_view_3: From this view, the barrels dominate the foreground, with the stock and smaller components arranged behind, giving a clear sense of the shotgun's overall length.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, no visible ribbing or venting, barrels of equal length
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metallic guard with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
